summary: Regression backend test post CORS refactor (hardcoded -> env-based CORS_ORIGINS). All 14 tests passed (9 existing + 5 new CORS-specific). No regressions detected.
critical_code_review_comments: CORS_ORIGINS='*' combined with allow_credentials=True works in Starlette (it echoes the request Origin back instead of literal '*'), so it is functional. However, for production deployments this is not recommended - specify explicit origins (e.g., https://rn-capital.preview.emergentagent.com,https://rnbusiness.com) to avoid inadvertently accepting cookies from any origin.
os.environ.get('CORS_ORIGINS', '*').split(',') defaults to ['*'] which is broad; consider trimming whitespace on each entry ([o.strip() for o in ...]) to make comma-separated lists robust to spaces.
JWT_SECRET has a hardcoded fallback in server.py line 32 - should fail-fast if env var missing in production. Not a regression, existed before refactor.
seed_data_creation: None (admin seeded automatically on startup). TEST_ prefixed data was created and cleaned up within lifecycle tests.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend fully green after CORS env refactor. Test file /app/backend/tests/test_cors.py added and validates preflight, ACAO on GET, allow-credentials=true, and env var presence. Existing test_backend.py covers health, contact, waitlist, login, /auth/me, submissions CRUD lifecycle, waitlist CRUD lifecycle, and logout invalidation.
rca of the issue: N/A - no issues found. CORS_ORIGINS='*' is correctly read from env and Starlette middleware handles the credentials+wildcard combo by echoing origin.
